steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The goal is to emphasize when and where *Argentum* debuted. Both the year (2018) and the location (New York City, New York) must be prominent, ideally at the front of the sentence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘As is common of Grimanesa Amoros’s sculptures, the iridescent multicolored LED domes of *Argentum* cut and imposing 253 cubic foot figure when the piece debuted.’: Buries the debut timing in a subordinate clause and omits the location entirely; emphasizes size and LED domes instead. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Artist Grimanesa Amoros often works with light in her sculptures, which tend to be large in scale.’: Mentions no specific work, no year, and no location; entirely off-task. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘In 2018, Grimanesa Amoros debuted *Argentum* in New York City, New York.’: Opens with the year (2018) and closes with the location (New York City, New York), putting both where the reader sees them first and last — exactly what the question requires. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘Grimanesa Amoros is a Peruvian American artist who often works with LED light in her large scale sculptures, such as *Argentum* [2018].’: Brackets the year as a parenthetical aside and omits the location entirely; does not emphasize when or where. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
